jobDescription:
Position Title : Manager, Engineering - Creator Growth & Onboarding
Location : Remote from the U.S.

About the role
We are searching for an engineering leader to manage a fullstack squad (iOS, Web, Backend) responsible for the user experiences and systems used to onboard and drive creator growth across LTK, one of the largest creator-guided shopping and brand marketing platforms in the industry! As the leader of one of our development teams, you will be responsible for conducting, leading and coordinating software development activities throughout the product lifecycle as well as managing the day-to-day activities of the development team within an Agile Development environment.

How you will make an impact
Provide technical and people leadership to guide your team’s development, reviews, and maintenance of new features, product requirements documents, technical design documents and functional specifications Influence the technical vision for your department through discussions with XFN peers and senior management. Owns the delivery process and Agile ceremonies, ensuring proper backlog refinement and value delivery in every sprint. Partner with senior management to identify appropriate metrics to monitor team performance. Drive a culture of experimentation, leveraging A/B testing and data analytics to validate hypotheses and optimize the creator onboarding and reporting funnels. Take an active role in hiring, goal setting, performance management, promotions, and retention strategies across your team. Minimize retention risks and create effective development plans to prepare employees for their next role. You focus on growing leaders – emphasizing all aspects of the employee lifecycle (recruiting, onboarding, performance management, and employee development). Work with the team and stakeholders to break down and prioritize work to reduce technical debt. Identify and pursue improvements in engineering and organizational process, gathering and using data to support and guide continuous growth in product excellence and engineering productivity. Drive integration and experimentation of enterprise AI tooling across your team’s workflows to realize new efficiencies and help LTK maintain a competitive advantage.

What you will bring to LTK
Leadership Qualifications:
At least 2+ years in an engineering management role with a proven and consistent history of leading, hiring, coaching, and performance management of developers.
Experience managing a fullstack team.
Proven experience in rapid, continuous releases, used to shipping multiple times a day
Able to drive productivity and motivate a team to full project completion
Detail oriented, with proven methods for tracking project delivery and managing-up progress
Proven ability to mentor and grow engineers across diverse technical backgrounds
Ability to build, maintain, and influence XFN product and design relationships to accomplish team objectives.

Technical Skills:
8+ years experience with software development, architectures and infrastructure.
Demonstrates consistent utilization of AI within daily workflows and actively experiments to discover novel applications to improve your reach.
Measures efficiency and quality gains from AI adoption, drives the feedback loop for continuous improvement based on the results.
Proven track record of delivering user-facing, high-visibility features
Deep expertise in at least one stack (Backend, Web, or iOS) with a working knowledge and the ability to influence direction across the others.
History of building highly scalable distributed systems and processes in a cloud environment
Experience working in a Dockerized environment.
Experience in building products and features in CI/CD environments.
Experience with AWS services such as ECS, Lambda, RDS, Redshift, Glue (among others).
Have built scalable internal and public facing APIs (RESTful, JSON, HTTP)

Nice to have:
Have built AI powered products, knowledge of affiliate advertising space, onboarding flows, user performance analytics and reporting.

What we offer
The opportunity to be part of the leading global company in creator commerce
A remote-first, productivity-first environment
Competitive compensation and benefits package to meet the needs of you and your family
401(k) with LTK company matching
Medical Insurance, Vision Insurance, Dental Insurance
Paid Maternity Leave and Paid Paternity Leave
Summer Fridays and Flexible PTO

About the job

Come make the world and accelerate your success. 

It takes great people to achieve greatness. People with a sense of purpose and integrity. People with a relentless pursuit of excellence. People who care about making things better For Those Who Make The World™. Sound like you? Join our top-notch team of approximately 43,500 diverse and high-performing professionals globally who are making their mark on some of the world’s most beloved brands, including DEWALT®, BLACK+DECKER®, CRAFTSMAN®, STANLEY®, CUB CADET®, and HUSTLER®.

The Job: 

As a IT Solutions Director you’ll be part of our Corporate Information Technology team working as a remote employee. The IT Solutions Director is a visionary leader responsible for setting and executing the strategic direction for IT solutions and platforms across a global enterprise. This role is pivotal in driving digital transformation and operational excellence by overseeing the entire lifecycle—from selection and implementation to ongoing management—of critical IT solutions. Ensuring tool governance aligns with business objectives, maximizes efficiency, and delivers measurable ROI, while maintaining robust vendor relationships and optimizing cost-effectiveness.

A core accountability is leading technology enablement within the IT operating model transformation, with special emphasis on infusing artificial intelligence capabilities into the IT solutions roadmap. By leveraging deep expertise in AI, enhancing automation, analytics, and innovation, while supporting enterprise-wide business growth and agility. The IT Solutions Director also provides governance and leadership for the ServiceNow enterprise platform and its key modules, ensuring seamless integration, process improvement, and compliance with security and regulatory standards.

This position demands strategic thinking, exceptional leadership, and the ability to manage complex technology ecosystems, ensuring the organization remains at the forefront of technology enablement and operational excellence in the global marketplace.

You’ll get to:



Establish and communicate the strategic roadmap for IT solution selection, implementation, and lifecycle management, ensuring alignment with organizational goals and digital transformation initiatives. 
Define and execute technology enablement strategies as part of the IT operating model transformation, optimizing critical platforms and driving enterprise-wide innovation. 
Lead enterprise-wide technology enablement initiatives, infusing artificial intelligence and automation into the IT solutions roadmap to drive innovation and business growth. 
Stay current with industry trends and emerging practices in product management, agile delivery, and digital transformation, continually evaluating and adopting new technologies to evolve the IT solutions landscape. 
Transform the ServiceNow environment into a robust, scalable enterprise platform, ensuring alignment with global business needs, supporting effective cross-functional collaboration, and establishing governance for key modules (IT Asset Management, IT Service Management, IT Operations Management, HR Service Delivery, Integrated Risk Management, Third-Party Supplier Risk Management) to drive standardization, collaboration, and strong governance practices and platform management. 
Spearhead the creation and governance of a Center of Excellence for SDLC tooling—including Azure DevOps, Jira, and advanced AI tools—setting strategic direction, integrating workflows, and promoting Agile and Product Management best practices. 
Establish the strategic vision for the Power BI platform, ensuring robust data governance, compliance with industry standards, and alignment with enterprise reporting and transformation goals. 
Lead the integration and enablement of Generative AI within Power BI, advancing analytics, automation, and AI-powered reporting to deliver actionable business insights. 
Develop comprehensive training and user enablement programs, fostering a data-driven culture and ensuring high standards of data quality, integrity, and stewardship across platforms. 
Lead governance processes for IT solutions and platforms, ensuring compliance with business objectives, regulatory requirements, and security standards. 
Implement and promote industry standards and frameworks to ensure best-in-class IT solutions governance, risk management, and operational excellence. 
Manage and empower cross-functional teams to drive process improvements, foster innovation, and implement technology solutions that enhance operational efficiency and agility. 
Oversee vendor relationships, negotiate contracts, and optimize IT solutions usage for cost-effectiveness and performance. 
Provide executive-level reporting and insights on tool performance, ROI, and digital transformation progress, leveraging data analytics and industry benchmarking. 
Oversee the selection, deployment, and continuous improvement of project and portfolio management tools and IT investment management solutions to maximize value delivery and align technology investments with business objectives. 
Drive change management and adoption initiatives, ensuring successful deployment of new tools and processes and fostering continuous improvement. 



The Person: 

You love to learn and grow and be acknowledged for your valuable contributions. You’re not intimidated by innovation. Wouldn’t it be great if you could do your job and do a world of good? In fact, you embrace it. You also have:

Required Skills



Strategic IT roadmap development and execution 
Technology enablement and digital transformation leadership 
AI integration and advanced analytics implementation 
IT solution lifecycle management (selection, deployment, optimization) 
ServiceNow platform governance and module management (ITAM, ITOM, ITSM, IRM, TPSRM, HRSD) 
SDLC tooling expertise (Azure DevOps, Jira, Agile, Product Management) 
Data governance, quality, and compliance (ISO, GDPR, industry standards) 
Power BI platform management and AI-powered reporting 
Vendor management and contract negotiation 
Cross-functional team leadership and collaboration 
Change management and user adoption facilitation 
Executive reporting, ROI analysis, and value delivery 
Continuous improvement and innovation evaluation 
Risk management and operational excellence frameworks 
Results Orientation and Accountability 
Problem Management / RCA 
Oral and Written Communications 



Education/Experience



Undergraduate degree and 10+ project management experience, or 
Graduate degree and 8-10 of years experience in IT Tools management or a related discipline 
Demonstrated leadership in the management IT tools and cross-functional teams. 
Proven expertise in IT Tools Management. 



The Details: 

You’ll receive a competitive salary and a great benefits plan, including:



Medical, dental, life, vision, disability, 401(k), Employee Stock Purchase Plan, paid time off, and tuition reimbursement in addition to programs & benefits in support of your well-being. 
Discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tools and other partner programs. 



And More: 

We want our company to be a place you’ll want to be – and stay. Being part of our team means you’ll get to:



Grow: Be part of our global company with 20+ brands to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths. 
Learn: Have access to a wealth of learning resources, including our digital learning portal. 
Belong: Experience an awesome place to work, where we have mutual respect and a great appreciation for a wide range of perspectives and experiences. 
Give Back: Help us continue to make positive changes locally and globally through volunteerism, giving back, and sustainable business practices. 



What’s more, you’ll get that pride that comes from empowering makers, doers, protectors, and everyday heroes all over the world. We’re more than the #1 tools company. More than a driving force in outdoor power equipment. More than a global leader in industrial. We’re visionaries and innovators. As successful as we’ve been in the past, we have so much further to go. That’s where you come in. Join us!

Please note that salary is only one component of total rewards at Stanley Black & Decker.

The successful candidate may be eligible for annual merit increases.

Discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tool sand other partner programs. 



We Don’t Just Build The World, We Build Innovative Technology Too.

Joining the Stanley Black & Decker team means working in an innovative, tech-driven and highly collaborative team environment supported by over 43,500 professionals in 60 countries across the globe. Here, you’ll get the unique chance to impact some of the world’s most iconic brands including STANLEY TOOLS, DEWALT, CRAFTSMAN, MAC TOOLS and Black + Decker. Your ideas and solutions have the potential to reach millions of customers as we work together to write the next chapter in our history. Come build with us and take your career to new heights.

Who We Are
We’re the World’s largest tool company. We’re industry visionaries. We’re solving problems and advancing the manufacturing trade through innovative technology and our Industry 4.0 Initiative. We are committed to ensuring our state-of-the-art “smart factory” products and services provide greater quality to our customers & greater environmental and social value to our planet. We are unique in that we have a rich and storied history dating back to 1843, but that hasn't stopped us from evolving into a vibrant, diverse, global growth company.

What You’ll Also Get
Career Opportunity: Career paths aren’t linear here. Being part of our global company with 60+ brands gives you the chance to grow and develop your skills along multiple career paths.

Benefits & Perks
You’ll get a competitive salary and a comprehensive benefits plan that includes medical, dental, life, vision, wellness program, disability, retirement benefits, Employee Stock Purchase Plan, Paid Time Off, including paid vacation, holidays & personal days, and tuition reimbursement. And, of course, discounts on Stanley Black & Decker tools and products and well as discount programs for many other vendors and partners.

Learning & Development: 

Our lifelong learning philosophy means you’ll have access to a wealth of state-of-the-art learning resources, including our Lean Academy and online university (where you can get certificates and specializations from renowned colleges and universities).

Diverse & Inclusive Culture:

We pride ourselves on being an awesome place to work. We respect and embrace differences because that’s how the best work gets done. You’ll find we like to have fun here, too.

Purpose-Driven Company:

You’ll help us continue to make positive changes in the local communities where we work and live as well as in the broader world through volunteerism, giving back and sustainable business practices.

All qualified applicants to Stanley Black & Decker are considered for employment without regard to race, color, religion, age, sex, sexual orientation, gender identity, national origin, disability, veteran’s status or any other protected characteristic.

If you require reasonable accommodation to complete an application or access our website, please contact us at (860) 827-3923 or at accommodations@sbdinc.com. Due to volume, we cannot respond to unrelated inquiries about the status of a completed application or resetting an account password.

Position Purpose:

Heritage is seeking a Director of Digital Product to define and lead the vision, strategy, and roadmap, delivering a best‑in‑class digital experience for professional contractors across our websites and mobile apps. This leader will shape the future of how contractors discover products, manage their accounts, and run their jobs through seamless digital tools. The role requires an understanding of contractor workflows and the ability to translate pain points and insights into innovative, scalable digital solutions. The Director will identify high‑impact opportunities, remove friction across the end‑to‑end customer journey, and drive measurable improvements in engagement, conversion, retention, and customer satisfaction. Partnering closely with engineering, UX, operations, marketing, sales, and analytics, this leader will champion the voice of the contractor and ensure our digital products deliver speed, reliability, and value that help professionals work more efficiently and grow their business

Key Responsibilities

Digital Product Strategy & Roadmap

Drive the vision, strategy, and execution of the Heritage digital roadmap, delivering an industry-leading experience for professional contractors.
Identify and unlock new revenue and engagement opportunities leveraging customer insights, analytics, and innovative product strategies.
Prioritizing features and enhancements based on business needs and customer feedback, and field insights.
Communicate product vision, progress, and results to senior leadership and cross‑functional partners, ensuring transparency and alignment
Ensure product decisions balance contractor needs, business objectives, and operational feasibility.

Product Delivery & Execution

Translate product strategy into clear, prioritized roadmaps and actionable plans that drive measurable business outcomes
Lead agile product delivery and cross-functional execution across product, engineering, design, and stakeholders to ensure aligned, on-time delivery
Own prioritization and trade-offs, balancing customer needs, revenue impact, and operational constraints
Ensure delivery excellence by monitoring progress, removing blockers, and improving team velocity and predictability
Establish and scale product management best practices, coaching teams and standardizing processes for consistent execution

Insights into Action

Champion a data-driven, user-centric approach, utilizing analytics to measure and improve digital performance
Define and track KPIs related to digital adoption, usage, order efficiency, account growth, and contractor satisfaction.
Use data to identify optimization opportunities and ensure continuous improvement of key funnels and user journeys.
Translate insights into product strategies, requirements, and prioritized backlogs.
Lead experimentation priorities with clear hypotheses, test design, measurement plans, and value assessment.

Customer Experience & Enablement

Lead customer and market research to deeply understand contractor workflows, pain points, purchasing behavior, and digital expectations.
Lead the redesign of the eCommerce sites and apps to deliver modern, intuitive, frictionless contractor experience.
Champion the voice of the contractor and advocate for simple, efficient, and aligned with real-world workflows.
Stay current on industry trends and best practices, incorporating new concepts into product designs and features.

Cross-Functional Leadership

Build trusted, collaborative partnerships across Marketing, Operations, Engineering, Sales, and Analytics, acting as the connective tissue for all digital initiatives.
Influence without authority, driving alignment around priorities, timelines, and desired outcomes.
Clearly communicate tradeoffs, dependencies, and decisions clearly to leadership team providing transparency and accountability.

Team Leadership

Mentor and develop product managers and design team members, to elevate product practices and foster an innovative, collaborative culture.
Build a high‑performing product function with strong execution, customer empathy, and cross‑functional alignment.

Direct Manager / Direct Reports

Reports To:

The Director, Digital Product reports directly to the VP of Heritage Digital, playing a critical role in executing the digital strategy across commercial workflows.

Direct Reports:

Yes

Travel Requirements

The Director, Digital Product will travel occasionally

Physical Requirements

This position operates primarily in a standard office environment with extended periods of computer work and virtual collaboration.
Field visits may require walking job sites, standing for training sessions, or observing contractor workflows.
Reasonable accommodations are available for individuals with disabilities.

Working Conditions

The Director operates in a hybrid work model, balancing remote work with on-site collaboration at SRS Heritage Group offices.

The environment is:

fast-paced
cross-functional
delivery-driven
highly collaborative
Success requires strong prioritization, clear communication, and the ability to manage multiple product streams simultaneously while driving meaningful impact.

Minimum Years of Work Experience

10 years of product management experience digital platform leadership, with 5+ years leading product teams; experience with core eCommerce capabilities in retail or B2B.
Proven track record building and optimizing eCommerce experiences that drive measurable business impact; B2B or digital distribution experience a plus.
Demonstrated success driving adoption, retention, and customer lifetime value through improvements to digital experiences
Deep expertise in one or more of the following: product discovery optimization, site search, product recommendations, checkout funnel, content strategy, distribution tools: quotes, estimates, project management, etc.
Excellent leadership and collaboration skills, with the ability to influence cross functional teams.
Strong strategic thinking with the experience translating business goals into roadmaps, KPIs, and actionable execution plans.
Analytical mindset, comfortable with KPI setting, performance analysis, and test‑and‑learn approaches.
Exceptional communication and presentation skills; able to articulate digital value to executives and partners.
Proven ability to lead through ambiguity, make strategy tradeoffs, and balance long- term investments with near-term business results.
Understanding of digital analytics tools (e.g., Google Analytics, Adobe, or similar).

Preferred Qualifications:

Experience building or optimizing digital experiences for trade professionals in industries like building products, pool, landscape, HVAC, plumbing, electrical, construction supply, industrial distribution, or related B2B verticals.
Deep understanding of contractor workflows such as quote‑to‑order, repeat ordering, job‑site deliveries, will‑call/pickup, and multi‑location account management.
Experience with complex B2B eCommerce capabilities including contract pricing, account hierarchies, custom catalogs, credit terms, punchout, and advanced search for technical products.
Executive‑level communication skills with experience influencing C‑suite leaders.
A track record of orchestrating cross-functional teams under tight timelines.
Experience working with ERP systems, PIM platforms, CRM tools, and other enterprise systems common in distribution environments.

Minimum Education

Bachelor’s degree required; advanced degree preferred.

Preferred Education

MBA or equivalent graduate degree in a business or technology discipline.

Certifications

Preferred but not required:

Certified Scrum Product Owner (CSPO)
Professional Scrum Product Owner (PSPO)
Agile Certified Practitioner (PMI-ACP)
Project Management Professional (PMP)

Competencies:

Product Management
Strategic Thinking
Customer-Centric Mindset
Data Driven Decision Making
Cross Functional Leadership

jobDescription:

Brinker International
Coppell, TX

What does it mean to be a BrinkerHead? It means creating moments that make everyone feel special — whether you’re supporting our restaurants, celebrating wins with your team, or sparking ideas that keep Guests coming back. We play like a team, take pride in our culture, and know that life’s too short not to work happy.

At Brinker’s Restaurant Support Center (RSC), every role fuels the success of our brands — Chili’s® Grill & Bar and Maggiano’s Little Italy® — and directly impacts Team Members and Guests. From bold ideas to everyday support, we help create a fun atmosphere, great food and drinks, and the kind of hospitality that keeps everyone coming back. Here, you’ll discover opportunities for career growth, belonging, wellbeing, and plenty of chances to work hard and have fun. Brinker International is an equal opportunity employer. We’re proud to provide a welcoming, respectful environment where everyone can thrive.

Job Summary
The Sr. Director of E-Commerce Engineering drives the development and optimization of e-commerce platforms for Chili’s and Maggiano’s focusing on delivering a seamless guest experience and revenue growth. This role is based in Dallas (Coppell), TX and follows a hybrid schedule (3 days in office). We are currently focused on local candidates or those open to relocating to the area at their own expense. At this time, we are unable to provide sponsorship support.

Your Key Job Functions
Lead, mentor, and grow a high performing team of software engineers, providing technical guidance and fostering a collaborative and results oriented culture.
Develop and execute the ecommerce, loyalty, and CRM platform strategy
Oversee the technical design and development of the ecommerce platforming, ensuring scalability, reliability, and security to handle high volume traffic.
Collaborate with product management to define technical requirements and delivery dates for new features and enhancements
Ensure best practices in software development, including code quality, testing and deployment processes.
Define performance metrics and success criteria for ecommerce engineering projects, ensuring high quality deliverables and efficient team operation.
Continuously monitor the performance, uptime, and speed of the ecommerce and loyalty platforms

What You Bring To The Team
10+ years of experience in Software Development, with a strong technical foundation in Node.js, React, React Native, CI/CD.
5+ years of experience leading software teams, with a proven track record of meeting commitments and delivering high quality software.
Extensive experience in building and scaling ecommerce platforms.
Experience with agile development methodologies
Bachelor’s degree in computer science, Engineering or similar field
Experience with cloud computing services - AWS and GCP
Experience working collaboratively with Product Teams.
Exceptional interpersonal, writing, and communication skills, with the ability to influence and inspire at all levels

At Brinker, we believe life is short, so work happy! That means creating an environment where you can grow your career, feel a sense of belonging and wellbeing, and have fun along the way. Here’s how we make that real for our BrinkerHeads at the RSC:
Flexibility that fits your life: Hybrid schedules with weekly flex days to work from home.
Benefits that support you: Competitive package with medical, dental, and vision coverage; life insurance; paid vacation and holidays; 401(k) with company match; Employee Assistance Program with counseling, financial, legal, and life resources; and Best You EDU, offering education programs and tuition reimbursement.
Enjoy our brands: Generous dining discounts at Chili’s® Grill & Bar and Maggiano’s Little Italy®.
Rewards for your impact: Annual bonus eligibility for every RSC Team Member.
Wellbeing at work: On-site gym and fitness classes like yoga and boot camp.
A culture worth celebrating: From company-wide events to a casual, collaborative atmosphere, you’ll find plenty of ways to connect, work hard, and play hard.

Identify and unlock new revenue and engagement opportunities leveraging customer insights, analytics, and innovative product strategies. Prioritizing features and enhancements based on business needs and customer feedback, and field insights. Communicate product vision, progress, and results to senior leadership and cross-functional partners, ensuring transparency and alignment Ensure product decisions balance contractor needs, business objectives, and operational feasibility. jobTitle: Director of Engineering, AI and Automation
analysis:
This candidate presents an exceptionally strong match for the Director of Engineering, AI and Automation role. Key strengths include over 20 years of software experience, with deep specialization in building and scaling AI/ML teams, and a proven track record of deploying production-grade agentic workflows and LLM applications at scale. The candidate's expertise in translating business objectives into technical roadmaps, enforcing architectural governance, and managing the full MLOps lifecycle directly aligns with core responsibilities. Furthermore, the candidate's background in 'clinical operational excellence' and 'clinical data governance' provides a robust foundation for SteadyIQ's B2G/high-trust environment and compliance requirements (SOC 2, NIST).

Potential areas for enhancement or to be prepared to discuss include explicitly highlighting experience with specific modern web application development technologies (e.g., React, Node.js, TypeScript) as mentioned in the job description. While the candidate's MLOps experience covers CI/CD, explicitly mentioning broader CI/CD, automated testing, and release management practices beyond ML-specific contexts would strengthen the profile. summary: Visionary Engineering Leader with 20 plus years of software experience, expert at building and scaling high-performing AI/ML teams for B2G and enterprise SaaS platforms. Proven for executing strategic technical roadmaps, deploying production-grade agentic workflows, and LLM applications at scale, enhancing development velocity and product quality across the software development lifecycle. Drives the operationalization of AI and automation into structured engineering workflows, fostering a culture of technical excellence and secure development practices aligned with SOC 2/NIST principles. Adept at balancing aggressive execution timelines with a customer-centric focus and collaborative leadership.
competencies:
\item \textbf{AI Strategy and Automation Leadership:} Expertise in translating executive business objectives into executable engineering roadmaps, setting the technical vision for enterprise AI/ML systems, and ensuring architectural governance. Drives the operationalization of AI, coding agents, and automation across the SDLC to move beyond individual tools into structured workflows, significantly improving development speed, QA coverage, and overall product quality.
\item \textbf{Advanced Agentic Engineering Workflows:} Possesses deep technical command over LLM technologies including prompt engineering, Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG), and multi-step reasoning. Specializes in designing and deploying production-grade agentic workflows and tool-use orchestration agents that integrate seamlessly into modern developer workflows for enhanced velocity and reduced manual work.
\item \textbf{Enterprise MLOps and Secure SDLC Governance:} End-to-end ownership from research and rapid prototyping through production deployment, experiment tracking, and cloud-native observability. Establishes and enforces robust standards for code quality, automated testing, CI/CD, secure development practices, and release readiness across frontend, backend, and QA teams.
\item \textbf{Cloud-Native Infrastructure and Big Data Architectures:} Advanced proficiency with cloud-native ML ecosystems across Azure (Azure ML, Azure DevOps pipelines) and AWS serverless computing alongside Python, Spark, Delta Lake, and SQL data architectures. Applies strong understanding of cloud infrastructure, DevOps practices, and CI/CD pipelines to ensure scalable, reliable SaaS product delivery.
\item \textbf{Team Scaling, High-Standards Culture and Responsible AI:} Dedicated to recruiting, mentoring, and managing engineering talent while cultivating a high-performing, inclusive culture focused on ownership, fast execution, and technical excellence. Implements responsible AI principles, clinical data governance, and secure SDLC practices, supporting SOC 2 and NIST-aligned requirements in high-trust environments.
